title: Doctrine of the Holy Spirit
summary: “God is a Spirit: and they that worship him must worship him in spirit and in truth” (John 4:24). God is a Spirit, the Lord Jesus stated. This is how we should conceive of God in his original essence. The Spirit of God is everywhere – omnipresent. David famously declared, “Whither shall I go from thy spirit? or whither shall I flee from thy presence? If I ascend up into heaven, thou art there: if I make my bed in hell, behold, thou art there” (Psalm 139:7,8). Furthermore, the Spirit of God is also all-powerful, omnipotent. The Spirit of God moved on the face of the waters during the creation week (Gen 1:2). God’s Spirit also resurrected the long-dead army of Israel in the Valley of dry bones in the vision of Ezekiel. Not least, the Spirit of God inspired the prophets to prophesy. Ezekiel 11:5 And the Spirit of the LORD fell upon me, and said unto me, Speak; Thus saith the LORD; Thus have ye said, O house of Israel: for I know the things that come into your mind, every one of them.
